general_editor.notes: Strong cold open with July 17 Census data. Clear thesis: AI budget misallocation from demand forecasting to marketing while sitting on 2007-level inventory. Original $7.8B calculation well-constructed. Structure flows logically: data → costs → alternatives → counterargument → policy → actionable advice. Limitations section honest and specific. Actionable section serves both builders and buyers.

voice_coach.notes: Em dashes: 0 (PASS). Banned phrases: 0 (PASS). The-starters: 0% (PASS). Sentence rhythm: PASS (variance 273.7). Frank DeLuca voice lands well: methodical, world-weary, measured. 'I checked them twice' is authentic character. 60.8% of sentences over 20 words is heavy on the long side. Article could use 2-3 more short punches for contrast. No AI tells detected.

ethics_reviewer.notes: No self-congratulation. Fair treatment of counterargument (geopolitical shock acknowledged as unfair standard). Doesn't demonize builders — frames as budget allocation problem, not stupidity. Doesn't oversell AI tools ('does not replace instinct'). Avoids preachiness.

social_shareability.notes: Strong headline with specific dollar figure. $7.7B pull stat is powerful. Multiple quotable lines: 'Marketing got the AI budget instead,' 'A better virtual tour does not fix a $25,000 price cut.' Buyer negotiation advice is strong share trigger. The paradox framing (shortage + glut) is inherently shareable.

legal_accuracy.notes: 21st Century ROAD Act correctly identified: became law Jul 12, 358-32 House, 85-5 Senate, without presidential signature. California AB 179 correctly identified as signed Jul 16. NAHB survey properly attributed to July 2025 HMI. Census/Reuters data properly sourced. No legal claims requiring case law citation.

research_rigor.notes: Novel contribution: $7.8B loss estimate against $4.5-9B universal tool cost. Cross-references NAHB adoption data with Pro Builder Top 200 spending patterns. Limitations section is specific: Sun Belt concentration, NAHB data age, BuildOps scope. Strongest counterargument (geopolitical exogeneity) engaged at full strength and only partially dismissed. Methodology transparent with inputs and assumptions shown.

data_presentation.notes: Pull stat well-placed. Numbers presented with clear context and comparison. Original calculation walked through step by step with inputs visible. Percentages consistently contextualized. Two-sided cost comparison (losses vs tool cost) is the article's analytical backbone and is clearly presented.
